_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MS/ DNB / PG Diploma in Obstetrics & Gynaecology (OBS&GYN)
MS/ DNB in General Surgery with documented reasons for non-availability of a qualified OBS&GYN specialist
clinical_indications: HELLP syndrome--H- hemolysis (breakdown of red blood cells)

preauth.label: Clinical notes clearly indicating reason(s) for being a life-saving procedure and performing caesarean hysterectomy
Lab investigations (Complete Blood count, Blood sugar- fasting and post prandial, Renal function test, liver function test, Urine- routine and microscopy)

claim.label: Discharge summary with follow up advise
Histopathology report of the specimen removed

ppd_preauth.q: Is youngest child more than 5 years of age?
Has hysterectomy never been done in the past?

cpd_claim.q: Does the patient have a definitive indication for undergoing Caesarean Delivery (e.g., Absolute disproportion, Chorioamnionitis, Maternal pelvic deformity, Eclampsia, HELLP syndrome, Fetal asphyxia, Umbilical cord prolapse, Placenta previa, Abnormal lie/presentation)?
Is there documentary evidence of indication of caesarean either from USG report of Gravid uterus or other reports like Non-Stress Test (NST)?
Do detailed operative notes include indications for caesarean hysterectomy and outcomes of the procedure?
Does the delivery note include outcomes of pregnancy?
Does the discharge summary include follow-up advise & status of the child at the time of delivery and at the time of discharge?
Are the picture(s) of gross specimen removed consistent with the Histopathology report of the specimen removed?

common_queries: Clinical notes are missing or do not clearly indicate the life-saving reason for caesarean hysterectomy.
USG Abdomen (Pregnancy USG report / scan) is missing or incomplete at pre-authorization.
The patient's gender is not female or age is outside reproductive age, as per system pop-ups.
Gravid Uterus not indicated, or Parity is less than 2, as per system pop-ups.
Detailed operative notes are missing or do not include indications for caesarean hysterectomy and outcomes of the procedure.
Gross specimen pictures or Histopathology report are missing or inconsistent.

clinical_key_pointers: The diagnosis made should be backed by clinical or Ultrasonography (USG) findings and all required reports shall be uploaded
Since the procedure is performed as an emergency life-saving procedure, there should be sufficient documentary indication and evidence to support it as an Emergency.
